prompt: A minimalist vector-style illustration on a flat light gray background showing a pelican riding a bicycle, drawn from simple geometric shapes with thick dark charcoal outlines. The composition is wide and centered, with the bicycle occupying the lower two-thirds of the image and the bird’s head floating slightly above and forward of the handlebars in a deliberately abstract, diagram-like way. The bicycle is a simple black road-bike silhouette in side view with exactly 2 large wheels, each with a thick outer rim and exactly 4 visible spoke lines forming a cross, a straight horizontal ground line beneath both wheels, a triangular frame, a short black saddle, and simplified crank and pedal details near the rear of the frame. The pelican is constructed from clean abstract pieces: a white rounded head with a small black circular eye, a long open beak pointing left made of 2 parts with an upper orange triangular section and a lower pale pink pouch section, a short dark curve on top of the head, a small separate white oval neck/body connector, a larger white rounded body positioned over the bike, and a single white wing-like shape angled upward over the back. Add exactly 2 orange leg shapes, long narrow rectangles leaning downward from the body toward the bicycle frame, suggesting the bird is pedaling. Keep the forms intentionally sparse and slightly surreal, like a logo test or simple code-editor doodle, with no shading, no gradients, no textures, no text, and lots of negative space.
